D6 Display L5 Evaluate Reference

HLG: Display-Referred HDR

broadcast HDR cannot assume a fixed peak — hybrid log-gamma bends its output to whatever brightness the receiving display can manage

PQ encodes absolute nits; Hybrid Log-Gamma is relative. Its signal is scene-referred, and a display-side OOTF with a system gamma decides the final luminance. That gamma depends on the display's peak: γs = 1.2 + 0.42·log₁₀(LW/1000). One broadcast signal therefore looks right on a 1,000-nit TV and a 4,000-nit one.

Slide the display's peak luminance and watch the whole transfer reshape — brighter panels get a steeper system gamma, not just a higher ceiling. Overlay the fixed PQ curve to see the difference between a display-referred and a display-independent standard.
